rulings.Case Title: JESSE BEVERLY JR VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Dismiss Action For Delay In Prosecution
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, October 19, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Rohr, Inc.¿s motion to dismiss for delay in prosecution is denied as untimely. Defendant failed to include 5 additional days for service by mail required by C.C.P. § 1005(b). = (220/HEK)

rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, October 19, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. The Hoge Fenton firm¿s motion to be relieved as counsel for defendant The Jack Dymond Company is granted. No opposition filed. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: KENNETH THORNTON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, October 19, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Underground Construction Company, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Plaintiff¿s deposition and the declaration of Mr. Riley create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos cement pipe attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Riley¿s declaration are overruled. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: SHIRLEE PATTON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, October 19, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 7. Defendant C.H. Murphy/Clark-Ullman, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its burden of production and plaintiff failed to present admissible evidence that she was exposed to asbestos attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to all references of asbestos content in the declarations of Mr. Gregorio and Mr. Recupero are sustained. = (220/HEK)
